input.problemStatement:
"show_save_as_new" in admin can add without this permission
Description
	 
		(last modified by Mariusz Felisiak)
	 
At "django/contrib/admin/templatetags/admin_modify.py" file, line 102, I think you must put one more verification for this tag: "and has_add_permission", because "save_as_new" is a add modification.
I rewrite this for my project:
			"show_save_as_new": not is_popup
			and has_add_permission # This line that I put!!!
			and has_change_permission
			and change
			and save_as,
input.predictedPatch:
diff --git a/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/admin_modify.py b/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/admin_modify.py
--- a/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/admin_modify.py
+++ b/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/admin_modify.py
@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@ def submit_row(context):
                 and context.get("show_delete", True)
             ),
             "show_save_as_new": not is_popup
-            and has_change_permission
+            and has_add_permission
             and change
             and save_as,
             "show_save_and_add_another": can_save_and_add_another,

goal: Fix permission bypass in admin's 'Save as new' button that allowed users without add permission to create new objects

hypotheses.claim: has_add_permission context variable is available in submit_row context (passed from changeform view)
The remaining conditions (change, save_as) correctly filter when button appears

risks: If has_add_permission is not set in the template context, button may be hidden or raise KeyError
If save_as feature has separate permission handling elsewhere, this change may be redundant

issues.message: Cannot verify exact line where has_add_permission is added to context in changeform view
Step 3 relies on general Django permission model knowledge rather than specific file evidence
